headline: Malaysia Recycles Only 33% of Scheduled Waste Despite 40% Target
context: DOE's 2024 report shows Malaysia recycled 33% of scheduled (hazardous) waste against a 40% national target, with 1.8 million tonnes requiring disposal annually.

cards.big: Malaysia generates 1.8 million tonnes of hazardous waste annually.
cards.sub: 33% is recycled. The target is 40%. The gap represents 126,000 tonnes of unrecovered hazardous material.

cards.big: Licensed hazardous waste recycling capacity utilisation is only 58%.
cards.sub: The problem is not facility shortage but logistics — 42% of capacity sits idle while waste goes to landfill.

cards.big: Scheduled waste disposal costs RM1,200-3,500 per tonne.
cards.sub: High costs incentivise illegal disposal. DOE detected 127 illegal scheduled waste dumps in 2024.

cards.big: South Korea achieves 72% scheduled waste recycling through extended producer mandates.
cards.sub: Malaysia's voluntary approach has plateaued at 33% for 4 consecutive years.

cards.big: A 33% recycling rate with 42% idle capacity is not a recycling problem. It is a logistics failure.
Connecting waste generators to underutilised recyclers requires better data, logistics subsidies, and mandatory waste audits. The facilities exist. The connections do not.
